subject: Product Management
title: Usage Frequency
description: A short lesson to help you apply Usage Frequency.

hook.text: How often users open your product tells you more about PMF than any survey ever could.

buildup.text: Daily active users divided by monthly active users (DAU/MAU) measures how habitually people use your product.

discovery.text: Facebook's DAU/MAU ratio is around 66% — meaning two-thirds of monthly users come back every single day.

twist.text: Not every product needs daily usage. A tax tool with seasonal PMF is fine. The key is whether frequency matches expectations.

climax.text: High usage frequency relative to the use case is a powerful PMF signal — people are choosing you over alternatives.

punchline.text: Users vote with their attention. Frequency is their ballot.

quiz.question: What does the DAU/MAU ratio measure?
options: How habitually users engage with a product relative to their monthly activity
